rationale: Both candidates catch the trust_remote_code True→False default flip (matches the acknowledged inline bug), but X also explicitly validates that endpoint-restriction resolution now follows the post-deploy-override pipeline — the single most substantive ground-truth concern (hsliuustc0106's confirmed bug) — and raises a plausible related caching/UNSUPPORTED_ROUTES gap. Y's extra findings (frozenset/set type mismatch, missing docstrings, 'temporary' wording) are grounded but mostly trivial nitpicks that don't overlap with any ground-truth concern (mutability of cached hf_config, positional-arg risk, debug-msg scope, create_error_response reuse, CI failures, cross-endpoint audit all go unmentioned by both). Neither has a LATENT GAP CHECK section.
